title: Knowledge Library Training Resources Index
desc: The Training Resources Index provides structured access to all employment law training content within the Knowledge Library. Training resources are organised by topic area and mapped to the relevant KL instruments, ACEI categories, and subscription tiers. Training content is delivered through the Knowledge Library Workspace (KLWS-001) and follows the Video Training & Content Architecture (VTCA-001). Content is curated from authoritative regulatory sources (ACAS, HSE, ICO, EHRC) and supplemented with Ailane-original educational content narrated by Eileen. Video content is hosted on YouTube (unlisted) and embedded via ID reference per the three-layer content architecture. All training content carries appropriate disclaimers per PLUGIN-001 Art. XIV §14.3.
disclaimerTier: Tier 2 (Standard) — Educational intelligence. Not legal advice. Seek independent legal advice for specific circumstances.

modules.title: What is unfair dismissal?
modules.desc: The statutory framework: ERA 1996 Part X. Qualifying period (currently 2 years, reducing to 6 months from January 2027). Automatically unfair reasons. The five potentially fair reasons.

modules.title: Fair procedure: the ACAS Code
modules.desc: The ACAS Code of Practice step by step. Investigation, notification, meeting, accompaniment, decision, appeal. The 25% adjustment.

modules.title: The BHS v Burchell test
modules.desc: The three-stage test for misconduct dismissals: genuine belief, reasonable grounds, reasonable investigation. Range of reasonable responses.

modules.title: Capability dismissals
modules.desc: Performance management versus misconduct. PIPs. The supportive approach. Medical evidence for ill-health capability.

modules.title: Redundancy as fair dismissal
modules.desc: The statutory definition. Fair selection. Consultation obligations. Suitable alternative employment. Statutory redundancy pay.

modules.title: ERA 2025: What changes?
modules.desc: Day-one unfair dismissal from January 2027. Compensation cap removal. Initial period of employment. What employers need to do now.

modules.title: The protected characteristics
modules.desc: Nine protected characteristics under EqA 2010. Direct and indirect discrimination. Harassment (three types). Victimisation.

modules.title: Pregnancy and maternity discrimination
modules.desc: EqA 2010 s.18. The protective period. Pregnancy-related absence. Maternity leave rights. Enhanced redundancy protection.

modules.title: Reasonable adjustments for disability
modules.desc: EqA 2010 s.20. The three requirements. The employer's duty to identify and implement adjustments. Examples in practice.

modules.title: Harassment and the preventive duty
modules.desc: EqA 2010 s.26 (three types). The new s.40A preventive duty (Worker Protection Act 2023). What 'reasonable steps' means. The 25% uplift.

modules.title: The burden of proof
modules.desc: EqA 2010 s.136. The two-stage burden shift. Prima facie case. Employer's explanation. Practical implications for investigations.

resources.title: TUPE Transfers: A Practical Guide

modules.title: What is a TUPE transfer?
modules.desc: Business transfers and service provision changes. The Spijkers multifactorial test. The organised grouping of employees.

modules.title: Automatic transfer of contracts
modules.desc: Regulation 4: how contracts transfer. What transfers and what doesn't. The pension exclusion. Employee objection.

modules.title: Information and consultation
modules.desc: Regulation 13: what, when, and who to consult. Employee liability information (28 days). The 13-week protective award.

modules.title: Dismissal and the ETO reason
modules.desc: Regulation 7: automatically unfair dismissal. The ETO defence. Changes in the workforce. Harmonisation after transfer.

modules.title: TUPE in FM: service provision changes
modules.desc: Sector-specific guidance for facilities management. Contract re-tenders. Multi-site operations. Practical compliance checklist.

resources.videoStatus: Planned — VTCA-001 production pipeline. Priority: high (FM sector relevance)

modules.title: What is a protected disclosure?
modules.desc: ERA 1996 Part IVA (PIDA 1998). Qualifying disclosures. The six categories of wrongdoing. The public interest test.

modules.title: Disclosure channels
modules.desc: Employer, prescribed person, wider disclosure. The escalation framework. When wider disclosure is protected.

modules.title: Protection from detriment and dismissal
modules.desc: ERA 1996 s.47B (detriment) and s.103A (dismissal). No qualifying period. No compensation cap. Interim relief.

modules.title: Building an effective whistleblowing policy
modules.desc: Policy requirements. Reporting channels. Investigation procedures. Protecting the whistleblower. ERA 2025 changes.

modules.title: HSWA 1974: your core duties
modules.desc: Section 2 (employees), section 3 (non-employees), section 7 (employee duties). 'Reasonably practicable' explained.

modules.title: Risk assessment fundamentals
modules.desc: MHSWR 1999 reg.3. Five steps to risk assessment. Recording and reviewing. Special assessments (new/expectant mothers, young persons).

modules.title: Workplace stress and mental health
modules.desc: Psychosocial risk assessment. HSE Management Standards. The employer's duty of care. Hatton v Sutherland foreseeability test.

modules.title: RIDDOR reporting
modules.desc: What must be reported. Specified injuries. Over-7-day incapacitation. Dangerous occurrences. How and when to report.

modules.title: HSE enforcement and penalties
modules.desc: Improvement and prohibition notices. Criminal prosecution. Unlimited fines. Director liability. Fee for Intervention.

modules.title: Lawful bases for employee data
modules.desc: UK GDPR Article 6 in the employment context. Why consent is rarely appropriate. Contract performance, legal obligation, legitimate interests.

modules.title: Employee monitoring
modules.desc: Email, internet, CCTV, GPS. Transparency requirements. DPIAs. Covert monitoring. The Barbulescu six-factor test.

modules.title: Subject access requests
modules.desc: UK GDPR Article 15. Responding within one month. What to search. Third-party data. The DPA 2018 s.173 criminal offence.

modules.title: Special category data in employment
modules.desc: UK GDPR Article 9. Health data (occupational health). Trade union membership. Biometric data. DPA 2018 Schedule 1 conditions.

modules.title: National Minimum Wage and National Living Wage
modules.desc: NMWA 1998. Current rates (April 2026). Worker definition. Common underpayment causes. HMRC enforcement.

modules.title: Unauthorised deductions from wages
modules.desc: ERA 1996 s.13. What constitutes a deduction. Exceptions. The 3-month time limit. Series of deductions.

modules.title: Working time and holiday pay
modules.desc: WTR 1998. Maximum weekly hours. Rest breaks. Annual leave entitlement (5.6 weeks). Holiday pay calculation.

modules.title: Statutory Sick Pay
modules.desc: ERA 2025 changes: day-one SSP from April 2026. Removal of LEL threshold. Low earner rate. Employer obligations.

modules.title: Maternity leave and pay
modules.desc: MPL 1999. OML (26 weeks) + AML (26 weeks). Day-one right. Notification requirements. Terms during leave. Right to return.

modules.title: Paternity leave
modules.desc: Day-one right from April 2026 (ERA 2025). 1 or 2 weeks. Can now be taken after shared parental leave.

modules.title: Parental leave
modules.desc: Day-one right from April 2026 (ERA 2025). 18 weeks per child. Unpaid. Blocks of one week. Default scheme.

modules.title: Redundancy during pregnancy and family leave
modules.desc: Enhanced protection. Priority right to suitable alternative vacancy. Protection from Redundancy Act 2023. Extended protection period.

modules.title: Flexible working requests
modules.desc: Day-one right. Two requests per year. ACAS Code of Practice 5. Two-month decision period. Eight business grounds.

modules.title: ACAS Early Conciliation
modules.desc: Mandatory pre-claim process. How it works. Stop-the-clock time limits. The EC certificate.

modules.title: Settlement agreements
modules.desc: ERA 1996 s.203(3) validity requirements. Independent legal advice. Pre-termination negotiations (s.111A). The ACAS Code.

modules.title: COT3 agreements
modules.desc: Settlement through ACAS conciliation. COT3 versus settlement agreement. Tax treatment. Scope of settlement.

surveillanceStatus.note: Training content is structurally complete (modules, topics, instrument mappings, durations). Video production and Eileen narration are Phase 2+ deliverables under VTCA-001. ERA 2025 commencement will require new training modules and updates to existing modules. The training index must be updated whenever the KL content estate is enriched.
pendingAmendments: VTCA-001 Phase 1 — YouTube channel setup and first video production
ERA 2025 commencement — new modules required for day-one unfair dismissal, zero hours, fire and rehire
Worker Protection Act 2023 — update TR-DISC-001 module on harassment for s.40A enforcement outcomes

governingArchitecture.layer: VTCA-001 — Video Training & Content Architecture; KLIA-001 Part III — Knowledge Library Content Architecture

constitutionalBasis.ACEI: ACEI Constitution Art. IV §4.1 — Training and awareness resources support employer readiness for ACEI-assessed compliance areas.
constitutionalBasis.RRI: RRI Constitution Art. V §5.1 — Regulatory readiness includes access to training on current and upcoming regulatory obligations.
constitutionalBasis.CCI: CCI Constitution Art. IV §4.1 — Conduct assessment rewards employers who invest in compliance training.

contentArchitecture.threeLayerModel: KLIA-001 Art. 8 — (1) Embedded KL article page (educational wrapper), (2) YouTube video (unlisted, ID-referenced), (3) Assessment/certification layer (future phase)
contentArchitecture.eileenNarration: All Ailane-original video content narrated by Eileen per EILEEN-001 and EILEEN-002 voice identity specifications.
contentArchitecture.disclaimerTier: Tier 2 (Standard) — Educational intelligence. Not legal advice. Seek independent legal advice for specific circumstances.
contentArchitecture.tierAccess: Training content accessible from Operational tier (£199/mo) upward. Scan tier users see preview content only.

governingArchitecture.productionStatus: All training modules are in Planned status pending VTCA-001 production pipeline build. Module structure, topic mapping, and instrument references are finalised. Video production, Eileen narration, and assessment layers are Phase 2+ deliverables.
